name: deEHR
description: Open-source patient-owned Electronic Health Record platform built on FHIR R4 and SMART App Launch standards, with selective anchoring on the Klever blockchain (no PHI on-chain). Targets LGPD, HIPAA, and integration with the Brazilian RNDS at the boundary.
description_good_justification: Repo description and README state purpose: open-source patient-owned EHR on FHIR R4 + SMART standards with Klever blockchain anchoring (no PHI on-chain).
interact_justification: Public GitHub Issues and PR discussions on https://github.com/brunocampos-ssa/deEHR.
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md covers Conventional Commits, signed commits, branch naming, PR process, mandatory security audit before each PR, and the hard invariants (no PHI on-chain, no real data in repo).
floss_license_justification: Licensed under the MIT License — released as FLOSS.
documentation_basics_justification: README.md provides project overview, architecture, status, and phase plan. CONTRIBUTING.md and SECURITY.md document workflow and reporting.
documentation_interface_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no external API exposed yet. Will apply in Phase 1 when the FHIR gateway and SMART auth server ship.
sites_https_justification: Project is hosted on github.com which serves all pages over HTTPS with HSTS.
discussion_justification: Public discussion via GitHub Issues and PR comment threads.
english_justification: Canonical documentation is in English. Brazilian-Portuguese (pt-BR) mirrors are maintained alongside per documentation policy in CONTRIBUTING.md.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained — repository created 2026-05-22; six PRs merged in the past week. Solo maintainer (brunocampos-ssa) responds to issues within days.
repo_track_justification: Git is used for version control.
repo_interim_justification: All intermediate commits are visible in Git history. Trunk-based development; squash or rebase merges per ADR-0003.
repo_distributed_justification: Git is a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Per CONTRIBUTING.md and ADR-0003 each future release will have a unique signed git tag (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).
version_semver_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and ADR-0003 specify Semantic Versioning.
version_tags_justification: Releases will be cut as signed git tags per ADR-0003. No tags yet because Phase 0 has not shipped functionality.
release_notes_justification: No releases yet — project is in Phase 0 (Foundations). Release notes policy is documented in ADR-0003 and will be in place before the first tagged release.
release_notes_vulns_justification: No releases yet and no known vulnerabilities.
report_process_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents the bug-report process (GitHub Issues with issue templates). SECURITY.md documents the vulnerability disclosure process.
report_tracker_justification: Public bug tracker via GitHub Issues with structured templates.
report_responses_justification: Solo maintainer actively triages all incoming issues. All Phase 0 issues raised so far have been addressed or are tracked with an owner.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement proposals are tracked as GitHub Issues and Architecture Decision Records (docs/architecture/). Examples: ADR-0001 through ADR-0005 — each opened, discussed, and Accepted or Proposed within days.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues and PR threads provide a public, archived record of reports, discussion, and resolution.
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md describes the responsible-disclosure process and the contact method.
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting is enabled (Security tab → Report a vulnerability).
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to acknowledging vulnerability reports.
build_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no compiled artifacts produced yet. Will apply once Go services and Rust/WASM contracts ship in Phase 1.
build_common_tools_justification: No build process yet (Phase 0). When introduced, Go services will build with the standard Go toolchain and Rust contracts with cargo + wasm32 target — both common FLOSS tools.
build_floss_tools_justification: No build process yet (Phase 0). Future build will rely only on FLOSS toolchains (Go, cargo, rustc).
test_justification: Phase 0 (Foundations) — no test suite yet because there is no production code yet. CONTRIBUTING.md mandates tests for any behaviour change once code lands (see test_policy).
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: No test suite yet (Phase 0). CI is already configured for documentation/security: .github/workflows/lint.yml (markdownlint, actionlint) and security.yml (gitleaks, Trivy fs, OpenSSF Scorecard); CodeQL runs separately via GitHub's default setup, wired into the main-branch ruleset. Test CI will be added when production code lands.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states: 'Add or update tests for any behaviour change.' Tests are required for code PRs.
warnings_justification: No compiled code yet (Phase 0). Rust contract crate skeletons pass `cargo check` cleanly with no warnings. Strict warnings will be enforced (rustc, clippy, go vet) when production code lands.
warnings_strict_justification: No compiled code yet (Phase 0). Project policy in CONTRIBUTING.md is strict warnings: gofmt, go vet, rustfmt, clippy with no lints.
know_secure_design_justification: STRIDE threat model per area at docs/security/threat-model.md. Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0001 to ADR-0005) explicitly discuss security trade-offs. Hard invariants (no PHI on-chain, no PHI in repo, security as a gate not a phase) documented in CONTRIBUTING.md.
know_common_errors_justification: Threat model and ADRs address common error classes per area (Identity Service, FHIR gateway, smart contracts, RNDS connector). CONTRIBUTING.md flags overflow, reentrancy, and WASM-specific issues for Rust contracts.
crypto_published_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped cryptographic code yet. ADR-0004 specifies Ed25519 (klv-1) for authentication/capabilityInvocation and post-quantum schemes via multicodec for assertionMethod/keyAgreement; all algorithms are published and well-known.
crypto_call_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code that calls crypto yet. Design (ADR-0004) calls only KVM-native verifiers (verify_ed25519, sha256) and crate-level Rust implementations of established algorithms.
crypto_keylength_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. ADR-0004 specifies Ed25519 (~128-bit security) plus a multi-codec PQ scheme for assertion/keyAgreement.
crypto_working_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specified algorithms (Ed25519, SHA-256, NIST PQ candidates) have no publicly known practical breaks.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specification excludes broken primitives (no MD5, no SHA-1, no DES, no RC4).
crypto_pfs_justification: Phase 0 — no key-agreement code shipped yet. Transport security relies on TLS (GitHub today; production gateway in Phase 1 will use TLS 1.3 with PFS-only suites).
crypto_password_storage_justification: Identity design (ADR-0001, ADR-0004) is passkey + DID-based. Passwords are not part of the authentication model — no password storage.
crypto_random_justification: Phase 0 — no shipped code yet. Specification requires CSPRNG (OS-provided) for key generation and nonces.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source delivered via GitHub over HTTPS / SSH; commits to main are GPG-signed (enforced by branch ruleset).
delivery_unsigned_justification: No package downloads yet (no releases). Commits to main must be GPG-signed (enforced by branch ruleset). Future release artefacts will be signed.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: No known vulnerabilities. No shipped functionality yet (Phase 0).
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: No known vulnerabilities. Private vulnerability reporting is enabled for fast disclosure.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: Gitleaks runs as a required status check on every PR (.github/workflows/security.yml). CONTRIBUTING.md prohibits committing real data, credentials, secrets, or tokens.
static_analysis_justification: No code yet (Phase 0). CodeQL is already wired into the main-branch ruleset as a required status check and will engage automatically when Go services and Rust contracts ship in Phase 1.
static_analysis_often_justification: No code yet (Phase 0). CodeQL will run on every PR once code lands.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Design uses only memory-safe languages (Go, Rust). No C/C++ planned anywhere in the stack.
osps_ac_01_01_justification: Personal-account repository (no organization). The sole maintainer account has 2FA enabled. GitHub's platform-wide contributor 2FA requirement also applies (rolled out 2022–2023 for users contributing code to GitHub.com).
osps_ac_02_01_justification: GitHub default for new collaborators is 'Read' — lowest available privilege. Permission changes require manual escalation.
osps_ac_03_01_justification: Branch ruleset on `main` blocks direct push: requires PR + 1 approval, signed commits, linear history. Direct commits are rejected at the server.
osps_ac_03_02_justification: Branch ruleset on `main` includes `restrict_deletions: true` — branch deletion is blocked.
osps_ac_04_01_justification: Both workflows (`.github/workflows/lint.yml` and `security.yml`) declare an explicit top-level `permissions: contents: read` default. Jobs inherit this read-only default; only the Scorecard job overrides it with the minimum scopes its action requires.
osps_ac_04_02_justification: Read-only `contents: read` is the workflow-wide default. The Scorecard job is the sole override and grants only what `ossf/scorecard-action` requires: `security-events: write` (SARIF upload), `id-token: write` (signed publishing), `actions: read`, plus `contents: read`. Verified per-workflow.
osps_br_01_01_justification: CI/CD workflows pin every third-party action to a full commit SHA with `# vN` comment (e.g. `gitleaks/gitleaks-action@ff98106e... # v2`). No untrusted parameters interpolated into shell.
osps_br_01_02_justification: Branch names are not interpolated into shell commands in any workflow. Workflows trigger on `push: main` and `pull_request` with no `${{ github.head_ref }}` injection patterns.
osps_br_01_03_justification: PR workflows use `pull_request` (not `pull_request_target`), so untrusted code runs without access to repo secrets. No workflow exposes `secrets.*` to untrusted code paths.
osps_br_01_04_justification: Same posture as br_01_03 — workflows validate inputs via the GitHub Actions context type system and avoid string interpolation of any external input into shell.
osps_br_02_01_justification: No releases yet — project is in Phase 0 (Foundations). When releases ship, ADR-0003 mandates unique signed git tags `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` per Semantic Versioning.
osps_br_02_02_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Future release assets will inherit the release tag identifier per ADR-0003.
osps_br_03_01_justification: All project-controlled official URIs (repository, issue tracker, vulnerability reporting) are on github.com over HTTPS with HSTS. The only `http://` URIs in repo documentation are HL7 FHIR canonical identifiers (e.g. `http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/...`), which are spec-defined identifier strings, not network endpoints.
osps_br_03_02_justification: No distribution channels yet (no releases — Phase 0). Future binary/package distribution will use signed releases per ADR-0003.
osps_br_04_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). ADR-0003 mandates release notes generated from Conventional Commits before any tagged release.
osps_br_05_01_justification: No build pipeline yet (Phase 0). When introduced, Go services will use `go.mod` (the standard) and Rust contracts will use `Cargo.toml` + `cargo` (the standard).
osps_br_06_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Releases will be signed git tags (GPG-signed commits enforced by ruleset); release artefact signing policy will be defined before the first release.
osps_br_07_01_justification: Gitleaks runs as a required status check on every PR (`.github/workflows/security.yml`). CONTRIBUTING.md explicitly prohibits committing real data, credentials, secrets, or tokens. No secrets present in git history.
osps_br_07_02_justification: SECURITY.md and CONTRIBUTING.md describe secret handling: GitHub Actions secrets for CI; no secrets committed to the repo; gitleaks scan on every PR; private vulnerability reporting for any incident.
osps_do_01_01_justification: No releases yet — project is in Phase 0 (Foundations). User guides will be added when the first user-facing component ships in Phase 1.
osps_do_02_01_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md and the GitHub issue templates describe the defect reporting process. SECURITY.md describes vulnerability reporting separately.
osps_do_03_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Future releases will include checksums + signed-tag verification instructions.
osps_do_03_02_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Identity is established through GPG-signed git tags (signed by the maintainer key); verification docs will be added with the first release.
osps_do_04_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Support scope/duration policy will be documented before the first release per the deEHR phase plan in README.md.
osps_do_05_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). EOL/security-update policy will be defined before the first release.
osps_do_06_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). Dependabot is configured for weekly dependency updates of GitHub Actions; dep selection/tracking policy will be formalised before the first release.
osps_do_07_01_justification: Phase 0 ships no buildable software — only documentation, ADRs, monorepo scaffold and Rust contract crate skeletons. Per-component build instructions will be added to each module README as components land in Phase 1+.
osps_gv_01_01_justification: CODEOWNERS at repo root lists the sole maintainer (@brunocampos-ssa) with access to all paths. CONTRIBUTING.md identifies the maintainer and their role.
osps_gv_01_02_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents roles (maintainer, reviewer, contributor) and responsibilities (code review, security audit before PR, ADR authorship). ADR-0003 documents the release/maintainer model.
osps_gv_02_01_justification: Public discussion happens via GitHub Issues, PR comment threads, and the Discussions tab. SECURITY.md provides a separate private channel for vulnerability reports.
osps_gv_03_01_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md describes the full contribution process: branching, signed Conventional Commits, mandatory security audit before each PR, PR template, Copilot review, maintainer review.
osps_gv_03_02_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md sets acceptance requirements: Conventional Commits, signed commits, tests for behaviour changes, security audit, ADR for major architecture changes, hard invariants (no PHI on-chain, no real data in repo).
osps_gv_04_01_justification: Solo-maintainer phase per ADR-0003. New collaborators with escalated permissions would be reviewed by the existing maintainer per the contribution review policy in CONTRIBUTING.md.
osps_le_01_01_justification: DCO sign-off is not currently required for commits. Will be added as a Phase 1 follow-up when external contributors begin landing PRs. Tracked for future implementation.
osps_le_02_01_justification: Source code is licensed under the MIT License — an OSI-approved license (https://opensource.org/license/mit/).
osps_le_02_02_justification: MIT applies uniformly to source and any future released assets. No re-licensing planned. ADR-0003 confirms MIT for the project lifecycle.
osps_le_03_01_justification: LICENSE file at repository root contains the full MIT License text.
osps_le_03_02_justification: MIT LICENSE will be packaged with all future releases per the standard practice (no releases yet — Phase 0).
osps_qa_01_01_justification: Public repository at static URL: https://github.com/brunocampos-ssa/deEHR. No mirrors or duplicates.
osps_qa_01_02_justification: Full git history is publicly readable. Linear history enforced; squash-or-rebase merges; signed commits — author and timestamp are preserved on every change.
osps_qa_02_01_justification: No production code yet (Phase 0). Rust contract crate skeletons declare an empty dependency list in `contracts/*/Cargo.toml`. Direct deps will be tracked there and in Go service `go.mod` when code lands.
osps_qa_02_02_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). SBOM generation will be added to the release pipeline before the first tagged release.
osps_qa_03_01_justification: Branch ruleset on `main` requires 4 status checks to pass: markdownlint, actionlint, gitleaks, Trivy fs. Admin bypass available for solo-maintainer phase (documented in ADR-0003 and tracked in #15).
osps_qa_04_01_justification: Single-repository project. No subprojects to document.
osps_qa_05_01_justification: No compiled/generated executable artefacts committed to the repository. Verified by Trivy fs scan on every PR. `.gitignore` excludes build outputs.
osps_qa_05_02_justification: No unreviewable binary artefacts in the repository. Trivy fs scan on every PR would surface any binaries. ADR-driven authoring discipline keeps the diff reviewable.
osps_qa_06_01_justification: No automated test suite yet (Phase 0 has no production code yet). CONTRIBUTING.md mandates tests for any behaviour change; CI will run them once code lands.
osps_qa_06_02_justification: No tests yet (Phase 0). When the first tests land, the convention (`go test`, `cargo test`, CI workflow) will be documented per-component.
osps_qa_06_03_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md states: 'Add or update tests for any behavior change.' Tests are mandated for code PRs and verified during review.
osps_qa_07_01_justification: Branch ruleset requires 1 approving review on every PR. Solo-maintainer phase uses admin bypass per ADR-0003 — to be dropped when a second maintainer joins (tracked in #15).
osps_sa_01_01_justification: Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0001 through ADR-0005) under `docs/architecture/` document the system actors, components, and trust boundaries. README provides high-level architecture diagrams.
osps_sa_02_01_justification: No releases yet (Phase 0). External interfaces (FHIR gateway, SMART auth, RNDS connector) are specified at the ADR level (ADR-0002, ADR-0005); per-interface API docs will land with the implementation.
osps_sa_03_01_justification: STRIDE threat model at `docs/security/threat-model.md` performs a security assessment per architectural area, with the Signing & Fee Service identified as the highest-value target.
osps_sa_03_02_justification: Same STRIDE threat model performs per-area threat modelling and attack-surface analysis: Identity Service, FHIR gateway, smart contracts, RNDS connector, app-layer custody.
osps_vm_01_01_justification: SECURITY.md defines the coordinated vulnerability disclosure process and a target response timeframe.
osps_vm_02_01_justification: SECURITY.md lists the security contact (private vulnerability reporting) and the maintainer email.
osps_vm_03_01_justification: GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting is enabled on the repository (Security tab → Report a vulnerability). SECURITY.md documents this path.
osps_vm_04_01_justification: No vulnerabilities have been reported or discovered. Future disclosed vulnerabilities will be published via GitHub Security Advisories per SECURITY.md.
osps_vm_04_02_justification: No releases and no known component vulnerabilities. VEX policy will be defined as part of the release process before the first release.
osps_vm_05_01_justification: No formal SCA remediation threshold policy documented yet. Trivy fs runs on every PR (`.github/workflows/security.yml`) so vulnerabilities are surfaced, but the policy threshold (block on Critical/High?) is not codified. Will be defined before the first release.
osps_vm_05_02_justification: Same as vm_05_01 — SCA violation policy will be formalised before the first release. Currently relies on PR review judgement.
osps_vm_05_03_justification: Trivy fs and Dependabot run automatically on every PR / weekly. Critical/High vulnerabilities surface as required status check failures, blocking merge. Suppressions are documented in PR descriptions when used.
osps_vm_06_01_justification: No code yet (Phase 0) — no SAST findings to threshold. CodeQL is wired into the main-branch ruleset via GitHub's default setup and will engage when Go services and Rust contracts ship in Phase 1; remediation policy will be documented at that time.
osps_vm_06_02_justification: No code yet (Phase 0). CodeQL via default setup is configured as a required status check on `main` and will automatically block PRs with new findings once code lands.
